verbatim_text: Okay so I'm going to attempt to address this question in two parts. In the first part I'll show evidence in favor of language models do possess internal world models and in the second part I'll address the question of how do they build internal world models. So first question let's try to answer this question by looking inside a language model. So when we give a language model a context like this how do we know if it's building a mini world model inside of its inside of it. So under the hood most modern language models are a transformer which looks something like this. They build vector representations across each token down a number of layers and each token at each layer attends to prior tokens from previous layers. So the representation at each token and layer is a high dimensional vector which we'll call an activation.
